content:
Hello everyone ,
I am trying to write some APIs that rely on some functionalities of the #include <linux/skbuff.h> header file in C , however when I try to test these APIs using make LLVM=1 rusttest , I keep getting these kind of errors :

"""
= note: /usr/bin/ld: ./rust/test/kernel.kernel.f2045623-cgu.15.rcgu.o: in function kernel::netlink::alloc_skb': kernel.f2045623-cgu.15:(.text._ZN6kernel7netlink9alloc_skb17he9fa8ee3f427d9a2E+0xa): undefined reference to __alloc_skb'
collect2: error: ld returned 1 exit status

= help: some extern functions couldn't be found; some native libraries may need to be installed or have their path specified
= note: use the -l flag to specify native libraries to link
= note: use the cargo:rustc-link-lib directive to specify the native libraries to link with Cargo (see https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/reference/build-scripts.html#cargorustc-link-libkindname)

error: aborting due to previous error; 7 warnings emitted
"""
Another remark , when I used make LLVM=1 in its own there are no such problems . As a newbie in the field I couldn't find any suitable solution for this problem . Thank you for your assitance !

If you mean #[test], you can't test kernel APIs with those -- they only have access to userspace. Instead, you can try a documentation test instead if that fits your use case. Please see https://docs.kernel.org/rust/testing.html.
